vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-34351
vulnerability.description: Next.js Server Actions SSRF via Host header manipulation

statements.impact_statement: PCI-DSS CRITICAL: The trading dashboard uses Next.js 14.0.4 with Server Actions enabled for real-time price data fetching. The Host header is not validated in the server action that fetches live price feeds from internal market data services. An attacker can craft a Host header pointing to internal GCP metadata (metadata.google.internal) or internal Spanner endpoints, exfiltrating service account tokens. This could lead to complete GCP project compromise.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade Next.js to 14.1.1 immediately. As an interim control: configure the reverse proxy (nginx/ALB) to strip and replace the Host header before forwarding to the Next.js server. Add network egress filtering to block metadata endpoints from Next.js server process.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-24795
vulnerability.description: HTTP Response Splitting in Node.js HTTP server via forwarded headers

statements.impact_statement: PCI-DSS: The Express.js BFF layer processes X-Forwarded-For and Forwarded headers from the ALB without stripping carriage-return/line-feed characters. An attacker can inject additional HTTP response headers into trader API responses, potentially injecting malicious redirect headers or CSP bypass headers. Can be used in session fixation attacks against authenticated traders.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade to Node.js 20.12.0+ which patches the underlying undici vulnerability. Alternatively, upgrade to Express 4.18.3+. Add middleware to strip CR/LF from all incoming headers using helmet.contentSecurityPolicy().

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-22019
vulnerability.description: Node.js HTTP keep-alive connection exhaustion DoS

statements.impact_statement: SOX/Availability: The BFF Node.js server can be forced to hold HTTP/1.1 keep-alive connections open by a malicious client, exhausting the connection pool. During peak trading hours, this could deny legitimate trader access to order submission endpoints. SOX controls require system availability during market hours. Impact: trading downtime = potential regulatory violation + client financial losses.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade to Node.js 20.11.1. Implement connection timeout (headersTimeout, requestTimeout) in the Express server configuration. Add AWS ALB connection draining settings to limit connection hold time.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2022-23529
vulnerability.description: jsonwebtoken RCE via malicious secret object with toString property

statements.impact_statement: jsonwebtoken 9.0.0 is the PATCHED version for CVE-2022-23529 (fixed in 9.0.0). The SBOM version 9.0.0 is not vulnerable. Previous versions (8.x) were affected. Additionally, the trading platform validates that the secretOrPublicKey parameter is always a string constant loaded from environment variables, never a user-controlled object.
statements.action_statement: No action required. Current version 9.0.0 is patched. Maintain version pinning. Consider upgrading to latest stable to pick up additional security hardening in 9.0.2.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2023-46233
vulnerability.description: crypto-js PBKDF2 1000x weaker than RFC 2898 specification

statements.impact_statement: Investigating whether crypto-js is a transitive dependency of Next.js 14.0.4 or any other frontend package. If used for client-side key derivation in the trading session management, the weak PBKDF2 dramatically reduces security for stored session keys. PCI-DSS requires strong key derivation for cardholder data access. Assessment pending dependency tree audit.
statements.action_statement: Run 'npm ls crypto-js' to identify which dependency introduces crypto-js. If present, upgrade crypto-js to 4.2.0+. If used for PBKDF2 key derivation, replace with SubtleCrypto.deriveKey() (browser native PBKDF2 with 600,000 iterations) or use server-side derivation via Node.js crypto module. Assessment due 2026-03-07.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2024-30255
vulnerability.description: Envoy proxy HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frame CPU exhaustion DoS

statements.impact_statement: The Go risk engine uses gRPC which uses Envoy proxy in the GKE cluster as a sidecar. Envoy version included in the GKE cluster is 1.28.x, which is vulnerable to the HTTP/2 CONTINUATION flood. An attacker can crash Envoy worker threads in the risk engine pod, causing trade risk calculations to fail. Without risk calculation, the trading platform should auto-halt trading per circuit breaker rules, but this represents a DoS against trading operations.
statements.action_statement: Upgrade GKE cluster's Envoy/Istio to 1.29.3+ which includes the Envoy 1.29.1 patch. Alternatively, configure Envoy to limit HTTP/2 CONTINUATION frames via per-stream limits. Verify risk engine circuit breaker correctly halts trading when risk service is unavailable.
